subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #39 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Thank You
As we come to the end of another school year, I want to express my sincere gratitude to each of our families for trusting us with your children. It is a tremendous honor and responsibility that we value every single day.
Thank you for sharing your children's laughter, curiosity, accomplishments, challenges, and growth with us. We are grateful for the partnership, support, and trust you have shown throughout the year. Watching your children learn, develop confidence, build friendships, and discover more about themselves has been one of the greatest joys of our work.
We are truly 
honored to be part of your family's journey and to play a small role in your child's story.
Thursday PEP Spirit T-Shirt and Pizza Cupcake Day and Friday Clap-Out
On Thursday, we will celebrate our final SOCDS PEP Spirit Pizza & Cupcake Day of the school year.
On Friday, we will gather at 11:30 a.m. for our annual Clap-Out celebration. Parents, please plan to arrive by 11:30 a.m. Parking can be a bit tricky, so I encourage you to allow a little extra time for arrival.
As always, I keep an eye on the street before we begin to make sure no one is still 
hustling down the block and misses the fun!
The map above shows where your child's class will stop so please feel free to stand near that area on both sides so we can dance past.
Children will be released to you from that area.  There is no lunch on Friday but please send in your child's backpack, a snack and water bottle.
Gardening with Ms. Linda
Butterflies are visiting SOCDS just in time for the late Spring flowers that have started to bloom. This week we discussed how these beautiful insects find their host plants. A black swallowtail butterfly was observed landing on each plant in the Perennial garden until it finally made itself at home on the rue plant; we later realized it had laid an egg. The rue plant belongs to the Apiaceae or Umbelliferae family which also includes celery, carrot and parsley, other host plants for Swallowtail butterflies. Females can recognize the host plants that will feed their very hungry caterpillars.
To prepare for 90-degree temperatures this 
weekend, we added mulch to our vegetable garden to help the soil retain consistent moisture levels.
​​If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child to be comfortable outside for 30 minutes at a time, including solid shoes for running.  Long, light shirts and pants protect from the sun and bugs.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your 
child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let 
me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
You are receiving this email because your child attends SOCDS.  If you no longer wish to receive these updates, please unsubscribe below.
